data.raceName: ERN JENSEN FUNERALS - PINDER FAMILY MILE
data.raceComments: {"comment":"The last leg of the quaddie is run over a mile, and includes many with each-way claims, including 2. KING OF THE SEA, who won his last 3 starts in the UK, and his jump-out form has improved when stepping up in distance. If this trip is not already too short for him, then he could have the last say. Check the market moves. 9. FIRST CHORUS is a very promising mare who has been well placed to win her last 2 starts, including her fresh effort at Pakenham 16 days ago over 1400m. The extra 200m is ideal, and the hat-trick beckons, especially if the track is wet. 14. TRAPDOOR was too good second up at Sandown-Lakeside 17 days ago, and will carry the same weight here. He has only been tried on wet ground and is in the right hands to take the next step. Each way. 17. NOBLER draws well second up, and stepping up to 1600m will suit him. Include in all top 4 combos.","runners":[2,9,14,17],"exclusive":true}
